title: Withdraw 120,000,000 ada for AlphaGrowth’s Cardano PRIME

rationale.summary: Vote: NEEDS_MORE_INFO. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (active), critical information needed for a responsible directional vote is still missing. Decisive reason: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 120,000,000 ada to fund Cardano PRIME for 12 months." and clears the evidence gates. What would enable a directional vote: Provide: Deep research dossier is required for treasury actions before directional voting; Provide: Complete proposal summary, budget analysis, feasibility, risks, alternatives, and failure-mode sections. Residual uncertainty: Dossier completeness not confirmed for this treasury proposal. Confidence context: score 0.00; confidence 20.0%.
rationale.summary_raw: Directional voting is blocked until dossier quality gates pass.
top_fixes: Provide: Deep research dossier is required for treasury actions before directional voting.
Provide: Complete proposal summary, budget analysis, feasibility, risks, alternatives, and failure-mode sections.
Provide independent evidence for: As of June 2026, Cardano DeFi has approximately $90 million in TVL and $45 million in stablecoin supply.
missing_evidence: Deep research dossier is required for treasury actions before directional voting.
Complete proposal summary, budget analysis, feasibility, risks, alternatives, and failure-mode sections.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Treasury withdrawal to fund AlphaGrowth's Cardano PRIME, a 12-month community-overseen program for DeFi protocol readiness, incentives, and liquidity growth across Cardano markets.

Recipient: 2026 Treasury Reserve Smart Contract stake address stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v, with Intersect managing withdrawn funds directly and AlphaGrowth executing program analysis and recommendations under Operating Group oversight.

Deliverables: Phase 1 public current-state audit, Phase 2 identification of integration, product, and ecosystem-support gaps, Phase 3 deployment of incentives and capital after Operating Group affirmation of the Phase 3 release plan, Published recommendations and disbursement records, Quarterly financial and ecosystem impact metric reporting, Independent audit or assurance funded by a dedicated 2,000,000 ada allocation, Separate auditable account for withdrawn funds, Return-to-treasury triggers for unused, unearned, unreleased, or excess funds
Deadline/expiry: 12-month program; Month 4 Phase 3 release gate
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 120,000,000 ada to fund Cardano PRIME for 12 months.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): PRIME is intended to improve DeFi protocol readiness, activate incentives responsibly, and grow durable liquidity across Cardano markets.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): As of June 2026, Cardano DeFi has approximately $90 million in TVL and $45 million in stablecoin supply.

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposer states Cardano's existing infrastructure additions have not yet converted into enough durable DeFi liquidity, application depth, LP participation, or institutional awareness.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The program uses three phases: a public current-state audit, gap identification, and incentive or capital deployment after Operating Group affirmation of the Phase 3 release plan.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal separates roles by having AlphaGrowth produce analyses and recommendations, the Operating Group review and potentially veto or condition material actions, and Intersect manage funds in a separate auditable account.
missing: Independent evidence for: PRIME is intended to improve DeFi protocol readiness, activate incentives responsibly, and grow durable liquidity across Cardano markets.
Independent evidence for: As of June 2026, Cardano DeFi has approximately $90 million in TVL and $45 million in stablecoin supply.

Independent evidence for: The proposer states Cardano's existing infrastructure additions have not yet converted into enough durable DeFi liquidity, application depth, LP participation, or institutional awareness.
Independent evidence for: AlphaGrowth has not received Cardano Treasury funding within the prior 24 months.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 120,000,000 ada to fund Cardano PRIME for 12 months." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "PRIME is intended to improve DeFi protocol readiness, activate incentives responsibly, and grow durable liquidity across Cardano markets." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.

actionable_asks: Publish a line-item budget: what is being bought, at what unit cost.
State the cost against the benefit: what does this buy, per ada spent.
Describe the funding path beyond this grant, or say plainly that there isn't one.
Disclose the rate basis — headcount, FTE fraction, or day rate — so the price can be checked against the market.
Tie payment to defined deliverables, not to elapsed time or headcount.
State your AI-cost assumption: this work is cheaper to produce than it was, and the price should show it.
affirmative_waste_evidence: independently verified excessive cost versus market comparables
missing: verified ecosystem benefit or strategic necessity
cost comparables, budget proportionality, or sustainability evidence

decision_contract.composite_formula: 40% ecosystem benefit + 25% delivery confidence + 20% cost efficiency + 15% downside protection - 0.50
decision_contract.benefit_formula: 18% critical infrastructure + 8% open source + 15% adoption + 12% ecosystem leverage + 10% strategic necessity + 10% prior delivery + 10% established service + 8% builder dependency + 5% low substitutability + 4% non-funding disruption risk
decision_contract.yes_rule: All quality floors, independently verified evidence, verified NCL, and portfolio capacity must pass.
decision_contract.no_rule: Requires affirmative independently verified waste/harm plus ecosystem benefit below 55%; missing evidence alone is never NO.
decision_contract.portfolio_gate: Verified on-chain Net Change Limit and opportunity-cost portfolio eligibility are mandatory for treasury direction.

treasury_capacity.caveat: Set by an Info governance action. Info actions change no ledger state and can NEVER be enacted -- they always expire -- so no formally ratified NCL exists or can exist. This is the strongest available public chain evidence: an anchor document byte-verified against its on-chain blake2b-256 hash, covering the current epoch, which won a majority of participating DRep stake. It is NOT enacted ledger state, and the majority may fall short of the supermajority other action types require. Published with every treasury rationale that relies on it.
